defs: The act or process of instituting; as: (a) Establishment; foundation; enactment; as, the institution of a school.
The act or ceremony of investing a clergyman with the spiritual part of a benefice, by which the care of souls is committed to his charge.
quotes: The institution of God's law is described as being established by solemn injunction.

defs: Established order, method, or custom; enactment; ordinance; permanent form of law or polity.

An established or organized society or corporation; an establishment, especially of a public character, or affecting a community; a foundation; as, a literary institution; a charitable institution; also, a building or the buildings occupied or used by such organization; as, the Smithsonian Institution.
Anything forming a characteristic and persistent feature in social or national life or habits.
quotes: The nature of our people, Our city's institutions.
We ordered a lunch (the most delightful of English institutions, next to dinner) to be ready against our return.

defs: That which institutes or instructs; a textbook; a system of elements or rules; an institute.
quotes: There is another manuscript, of above three hundred years old, . . . being an institution of physic.
